'''Jon Clarke''' (born 4 April 1979) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] player who played as a {{rlp|hk}}. Jon is currently Head of Performance at Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles in the NRL.He was previously the strength & conditioning coach for the [[England national rugby union team|England]] and [[British & Irish Lions]] [[rugby union]] teams. He played for the [[Wigan Warriors]], [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Widnes Vikings]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
He represented Great Britain Academy as [[Captain (sports)|captain]] whilst coming through the junior ranks at Wigan Warriors, he played in their 1997 Premiership Final winning side against St. Helens. He followed his Wigan Warriors coach [[John Monie]] to the London Broncos in 2000, but returned north in 2001 when the Warrington Wolves were forced to sign a {{rlp|hk}} on loan to solve an injury crisis.{{cn|date=March 2023}}

Generally used as Warrington Wolves' starting {{rlp|hk}}, he was Super League's most effective tackler in 2004 with a 99% success rate, and was fourth-highest tackler in the competition in 2005.

In June 2007, Clarke was called up to the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] squad for the [[test match (rugby league)|Test match]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6744909.stm|title=Eight new faces in Lions squad|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-12|access-date=2007-06-12}}</ref>

The Warrington Wolves fans voted him 'Greatest Hooker' in the club's history, beating John Thursfield and Mark Roskell into 2nd and 3rd place.{{cn|date=July 2021}}

He was forced to rule himself out contention for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] through injury.<ref name="Deacon pulls out of England squad">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7604682.stm|title=Deacon pulls out of England squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-09-16|access-date=2008-10-03}}</ref>

Clarke played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live |df=dmy }}</ref>

It was announced on 6 September 2011 that Clarke would be joining [[Widnes Vikings]] in their forthcoming return to [[Super League]].

==Coaching career==
Clarke retired at the end of the 2014 season, and joined the coaching staff at Widnes.<ref>{{cite web|title=Jon Clarke added to Widnes Vikings coaching staff|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28974187|website=BBC Sport|access-date=25 January 2015|date=28 August 2014}}</ref>

Clarke was on the coaching staff of [[England national rugby union team|England RU]] at the [[2019 Rugby World Cup]] in Japan. He was a strength and conditioning coach on the [[2021 British & Irish Lions tour to South Africa]].

On 28 Nov 2023 it was reported that he had taken up a role as head of performance at the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=28 Nov 2023|title=Jon Clarke returns to rugby league in role with NRL club Manly Sea Eagles: ‘An unbelievable opportunity’|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jon-clarke-returns-to-rugby-league-in-role-with-nrl-club-manly-sea-eagles-an-unbelievable-opportunity}}</ref>
